Gomesa macropetala belongs to the genus Gomesa, which is a group of orchids that are commonly found in tropical regions. The genus Gomesa is further divided into several species, with Gomesa macropetala being one of the most unique due to its distinctive petals. The classification of Gomesa macropetala is subject to ongoing research and taxonomic revisions.

intro: Gomesa macropetala, also known as the large-petaled Gomesa or the Lion's Head Orchid, is a species of orchid native to Brazil. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits of reducing fever and alleviating respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Gomesa macropetala include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
